title: Sheinbaum Announces 'Plan Michoacán for Peace and Justice': 57 Billion Peso 12-Point Strategy
detail: President Sheinbaum unveiled 'Plan Michoacán for Peace and Justice,' a 57-billion-peso (~US$3.1 billion) 12-point strategy targeting Michoacán state, which has seen sustained cartel violence from CJNG and the CSRL faction. The plan was announced against a backdrop of street protests in Uruapan, Morelia, and other Michoacán cities demanding security improvements. It includes approximately 2,000 additional federal troops and National Guard deployments, alongside investment in economic development, housing, healthcare, education, and institutional strengthening. Sheinbaum framed it as a comprehensive approach: 'Security is not achieved with wars, but with justice, development and respect for life.' She committed to monthly public progress reports at the mañanera through 2030. Michoacán remains one of the six Mexican states where homicide counts rose rather than declined during Sheinbaum's first year, with CJNG territorial expansion driving persistent violence.

media.caption: Sheinbaum announces Plan Michoacan 57 billion peso security strategy

title: Buenavista–AIFA Suburban Train Inauguration Delayed Again Over Safety Certification Gap
detail: Sheinbaum confirmed at her mañanera that the flagship Buenavista–AIFA suburban train link — a 43-minute airport rail connection — is delayed indefinitely beyond its original Holy Week target of March 29, 2026, citing a missing safety certification. An April 6 target also slipped. The project, expected to be operational before the June 2026 FIFA World Cup, is a high-priority infrastructure initiative. No new opening date was provided; Sheinbaum asked Mexicana de Aviación to study direct flight options to Hermosillo as a workaround for connectivity gaps. The repeated delays have drawn criticism from opposition legislators who cite the episode as emblematic of project management weaknesses.

title: Electoral Reform 'Plan B' Emerges After Congress Defeats Sheinbaum's Original Bill
detail: After her first electoral reform bill was rejected in Congress on March 10, 2026 (259 votes for, 234 against — 71 short of the required two-thirds supermajority, with even coalition partners PVEM and PT voting against it), a revised 'Plan B' electoral reform proposal emerged. Under Plan B, a presidential recall election could be held in 2028 rather than 2027 as originally pledged by Sheinbaum. The defeat of the original reform exposed the limits of Morena's legislative control: despite holding a formal supermajority, key coalition partners refused to support the electoral reform package. Analysts at AS/COA noted the episode signals growing coalition fractures on politically sensitive legislation, even as the government advances on security and infrastructure fronts.

title: Mexico Measles Outbreak: 17.2 Million Vaccines Administered; 14,869 Confirmed Cases, 35 Deaths
detail: At her Tuesday mañanera, President Sheinbaum provided an update on Mexico's ongoing measles outbreak, which began in February 2025. As of March 31, 2026: 17.2 million vaccines have been administered nationally, 14,869 cases confirmed across 457 municipalities, and 35 deaths recorded. The government is targeting full vaccination coverage and reports 97%+ medicine supply rates across IMSS, IMSS-Bienestar, and ISSSTE. Health Secretary Kershenobich has described the outbreak as contained but not yet eliminated, with unvaccinated rural communities remaining the primary transmission vector. The government also confirmed a Río Sonora remediation settlement: Grupo México deposited 1.559 billion pesos into the remediation fund, bringing the total to 2.222 billion pesos from federal, state, and labor tribunal contributions.
